title: thymus serpyllum f. collivagus
intro: Thymus serpyllum f. collivagus, also known as wild thyme, is a subspecies of the thyme plant, a low-growing, flowering herb native to Europe, North Africa, and Western Asia. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include being anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal. The health benefits of Thymus serpyllum f. collivagus include relief from digestive issues, coughs, and colds, as well as its potential to reduce stress and promote better sleep. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and essential oils, which can be used to treat various health issues, although it's recommended to consult a healthcare professional before using it medicinally.
